meaning: adj.始新世的,第三纪下层的n.[地]始新世,第三纪下层
examples: The other was a feathered skull from the Eocene (55m years ago) that had been unearthed in Denmark.
Few or any anthropoids are known to have existed in Africa during this period, known as the Eocene epoch.
There was a similar spike in carbon and temperatures in the Eocene- some 15 millions after.
The fossils ranged from small Eocene swimmers imprinted on rock to the remains of late-Cretaceous dinosaurs.
